mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by non-negative 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's 3-year rolling hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth accelerates while HOUST remains depressed, the combination indicates consumption holding up despite weak housing, supporting earnings breadth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform each single series alone over 60-day returns, or if combined-signal Rank IC is below 0.04 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO trend indicates improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 54% of cases, or if the sign relationship reverses in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in CPIAUCSL after prior inflation pressure lowers the expected policy path and real discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of disinflation-triggered observations, or if the trigger loses predictive value after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains in PAYEMS together with a falling UNRATE indicate labor-market expansion, stronger consumption, and higher expected earnings, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor signal does not beat either proxy alone on 60-day forward returns, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, tightening becomes restrictive enough to compress equity multiples and slow demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS remained near 0.66% in 2017 and the hypothesis activates only once policy becomes restrictive
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold episodes still produce positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the threshold effect disappears in rolling 3-year evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in GDPC1 growth together with rising RSAFS indicates synchronized real activity and nominal spending, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDP-sales condition does not outperform the individual series on 60-day returns, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of aligned-growth observations.
